← 返回文章列表

NASA Announces Artemis III Crew for Historic Lunar Mission Test

📅 2026年6月23日 📖 NASA/ESA 📝 约850词 ⭐ 难度适中
原文出处： https://www.esa.int/ESA_Multimedia/Images/2026/06/Artemis_III_crew_announcement
文章摘要：NASA正式公布了Artemis III任务的宇航员名单，包括ESA宇航员Luca Parmitano担任飞行员。该任务将在2027年进行为期两周的地球轨道测试，验证与商业月球着陆器的交会对接能力，为未来的登月任务奠定基础。
📖 英文正文

NASA has officially announced the crew for the Artemis III mission, marking a significant milestone in humanity's return to the Moon. The four astronauts selected for this historic mission will test critical capabilities needed for future lunar landings.

The crew consists of Randy Bresnik as commander, Luca Parmitano of ESA (European Space Agency) as pilot, and mission specialists Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas. NASA astronaut Bob Hines was named as the backup crew member. This announcement took place at NASA's Johnson Space Center in Houston on June 9, 2026.

Artemis III is scheduled for 2027 and will be a roughly two-week mission in low Earth orbit. The primary objective is to demonstrate the Orion spacecraft's rendezvous and docking capabilities with test versions of commercial human landing systems being developed by Blue Origin and SpaceX.

"This mission represents a critical step in our journey back to the Moon," said NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman. "The Artemis III astronauts will test the systems that will one day carry humans to the lunar surface and eventually to Mars."

ESA astronaut Luca Parmitano's selection marks the first time a European astronaut has been assigned as pilot for an Artemis mission. ESA is also providing its third European Service Module for Orion, which provides essential capabilities including power, propulsion, and thermal control.

"Luca's assignment reflects the depth of European expertise in human spaceflight," said Josef Aschbacher, ESA's Director General. "Europeans can take pride in being part of this exciting journey back to the Moon."

The mission will launch aboard NASA's SLS (Space Launch System) rocket from Kennedy Space Center in Florida. Once in orbit, Orion will perform unprecedented docking operations with both Blue Origin's Blue Moon lander prototype and SpaceX's Starship pathfinder.

The crew will now begin rigorous training on Orion systems and the operations of the human landing systems. This training will prepare them for an ambitious series of demonstrations that will pave the way for Artemis IV, the first planned crewed mission to the Moon's South Pole in 2028.

Randy Bresnik, a retired U.S. Marine colonel, previously flew aboard space shuttle Atlantis and served as commander of Expedition 53 on the International Space Station. Luca Parmitano has completed two spaceflights and became the first Italian to command the ISS during Expedition 61.

Frank Rubio holds the record for the longest single space mission by an American astronaut, spending 371 days in orbit. Andre Douglas will be making his first trip to space after being selected as a NASA astronaut in 2021.

The Artemis program represents NASA's ambitious plan to establish a long-term human presence on the Moon and prepare for future crewed missions to Mars. Each mission builds upon the previous one, testing increasingly complex systems and procedures.

📚 重点词汇表

词汇等级说明：★★★★★核心词汇 | ★★★★高频词汇 | ★★★常见词汇 | ★★拓展词汇

词汇 音标 词性 释义 等级 例句
announce /əˈnaʊns/ v. 宣布，宣告 ★★★★★ NASA announced the crew.
mission /ˈmɪʃn/ n. 任务，使命 ★★★★★ The mission will launch in 2027.
astronaut /ˈæstrənɔːt/ n. 宇航员 ★★★★★ Four astronauts were selected.
demonstrate /ˈdemənstreɪt/ v. 展示，证明 ★★★★ They will demonstrate docking.
capability /ˌkeɪpəˈbɪləti/ n. 能力，功能 ★★★★ Test critical capabilities.
rendezvous /ˈrendɪvuː/ n./v. 会合， rendezvous ★★ Orion will rendezvous with landers.
docking /ˈdɒkɪŋ/ n. 对接 ★★ Docking operations are critical.
rigorous /ˈrɪɡərəs/ adj. 严格的，严谨的 ★★★ Rigorous training begins.
propulsion /prəˈpʌlʃn/ n. 推进，推进力 ★★ Provides propulsion for Orion.
milestone /ˈmaɪlstəʊn/ n. 里程碑 ★★★★ A significant milestone.
humanity /hjuˈmænəti/ n. 人类 ★★★★ Humanity's return to the Moon.
expertise /ˌekspɜːˈtiːz/ n. 专业知识，专长 ★★★ European expertise in spaceflight.
ambitious /æmˈbɪʃəs/ adj. 有雄心的， ambitious ★★★★ An ambitious series of tests.
establish /ɪˈstæblɪʃ/ v. 建立，设立 ★★★★★ Establish a presence on the Moon.
prepare /prɪˈpeər/ v. 准备 ★★★★★ Prepare for missions to Mars.
🔍 长难句解析
NASA has officially announced the crew for the Artemis III mission, marking a significant milestone in humanity's return to the Moon.
【语法考点】现在分词作状语 • 主句：NASA has officially announced the crew... • 现在分词状语：marking a significant milestone...（表示结果/伴随动作） • 介词短语：in humanity's return to the Moon（修饰milestone）
The four astronauts selected for this historic mission will test critical capabilities needed for future lunar landings.
【语法考点】过去分词作后置定语 • 主语：The four astronauts • 过去分词定语1：selected for this historic mission（修饰astronauts） • 过去分词定语2：needed for future lunar landings（修饰capabilities）
ESA is also providing its third European Service Module for Orion, which provides essential capabilities including power, propulsion, and thermal control.
【语法考点】定语从句 + 介词短语 • 主句：ESA is also providing its third European Service Module... • 定语从句：which provides essential capabilities...（which指代Service Module） • 介词短语：including power, propulsion, and thermal control（修饰capabilities）
The Artemis program represents NASA's ambitious plan to establish a long-term human presence on the Moon and prepare for future crewed missions to Mars.
【语法考点】不定式作定语 + 并列结构 • 主句：The Artemis program represents NASA's ambitious plan... • 不定式定语：to establish... and prepare...（修饰plan） • 并列不定式：establish和prepare并列
✍️ 高考写作素材积累

以下表达可用于高考作文，关联话题：科技发展、国际合作、青年责任、梦想追求

mark a significant milestone in... 【科技发展/历史事件】
标志着...的重要里程碑，用于描述重大成就
humanity's return to... 【科技发展/人类进步】
人类重返...，用于描述探索与进步
reflects the depth of expertise in... 【国际合作/专业能力】
体现了在...领域的深厚专业知识
pave the way for... 【科技发展/未来规划】
为...铺平道路，用于描述基础性工作
establish a long-term presence on... 【科技发展/战略规划】
在...建立长期存在，用于描述长期目标
Each mission builds upon the previous one. 【科技发展/循序渐进】
每次任务都建立在前一次的基础上，用于描述渐进式发展
💬 讨论问题
1. Why is international cooperation important in space exploration programs like Artemis?
中文翻译：为什么国际合作在像Artemis这样的太空探索项目中很重要？
答题提示：可从资源共享、技术互补、降低成本、促进和平等角度分析。ESA参与体现了国际合作的价值。
2. What challenges might astronauts face during the Artemis III mission?
中文翻译：宇航员在Artemis III任务中可能面临哪些挑战？
答题提示：可从技术挑战（对接操作）、训练强度、心理压力、安全风险等方面展开。
3. How does the Artemis program contribute to humanity's future in space exploration?
中文翻译：Artemis项目如何为人类未来的太空探索做出贡献？
答题提示：可从建立月球基地、验证技术、为火星任务做准备、推动科技进步等方面分析。

📅 本文日期：2026年6月23日 | 适合高三学生备考使用
